title: REBX: Commerce Systems & Multi-Channel Ops
summary: Built the in-house systems RebX runs on — bulk listing, custom dual-shop Shopify, trade-in platform, Daily Returns Board, and marketplace integrations — while managing Temu, eBay, Back Market, and DTC.
cardSummary: 12+ in-house systems at RebX — bulk listing, sole-built Shopify, returns ops, trade-in, Temu ~£4K/mo.
challenge: RebX scaled refurbished volume faster than off-the-shelf tools could support. Listing, returns, trade-in, and multi-channel catalogue work needed in-house infrastructure — not another agency build or spreadsheet workaround.
challengeParagraphs: RebX scaled refurbished volume faster than off-the-shelf tools could support. Listing staff could not expand without bulk listing infrastructure; returns needed org-wide SLA enforcement; Shopify needed a dual Refurbished + Bargains architecture built in-house.

Marketplace channels each had distinct constraints — Temu required 4Seller integration and bulk listing work listing staff would not take on; Amazon Renew needed grade-accurate BaseLinker feeds; eBay returns needed API-linked intake instead of manual transcription between warehouse and CS.
strategy: Prioritise internal systems that remove operational bottlenecks first — bulk listing, returns board, trade-in — then layer channel integrations (4Seller, BaseLinker, eBay API) so each marketplace can run without misselling grades or manual handoffs.
implementation: Built bulk listing system — listing team depends on it entirely; business scale and hiring more listers impossible without it
Sole owner of custom Shopify — dual Refurbished + Bargains shops, Liquid/CSS theme, collection architecture, StoreFeeder, payments, ongoing technical management, no agency
Conceived and built custom trade-in platform in-house — GDPR-compliant, customer data encrypted to industry best practice
Built Daily Returns Board with SLA enforcement org-wide; Returns Label Scanner in progress — OCR reads labels, eBay Returns API pulls case data, feeds structured tasks with deadlines
Configured 4Seller for Temu refurbished storefront and personally bulk-listed new-condition items after listing staff declined the channel — ~£4K/month run rate
Set up TikTok Shop from scratch — £3,500/month in month one with zero ad spend (channel subsequently lost while I was away)
Configured BaseLinker for Amazon Renew grade-accurate listing — only attempt to make Renew viable without misselling risk
Built eBay image converter resolving a platform-wide hosting issue IT and eBay could not fix
Built Back Market trade-in tracker with reporting bridging tech room and management
Built Jarvis WMS asset checker and SKU image fetcher used daily by listing team
Marginal VAT calculation tool in progress — pulls cross-channel sales by PO for shareholder/accountant reporting
Initiated eBay Live setup — intro call with eBay Live manager, host sourcing in progress

operationsPrograms.title: Bulk listing system
operationsPrograms.problem: Processed stock could not reach marketplaces at the rate the business required. Without bulk listing, hiring more listing staff would not help — the bottleneck made expansion unviable.
operationsPrograms.approach: Built and maintained bulk listing infrastructure the listing team runs on daily. Current RebX listing operations depend on this system entirely.
highlights: Core dependency for listing team — none of current listing volume possible without it
Enables processed stock to be listed at operational scale across channels
Removing this system would block business growth and new listing headcount

screenshots.caption: Bulk listing workflow
screenshots.capture: Screen showing bulk listing interface or output — processed stock moving to live listings at scale. Redact sensitive SKUs or pricing if needed.

operationsPrograms.title: Daily Returns Board
operationsPrograms.problem: Returns were transcribed manually between warehouse intake and CS. SLAs were missed; no single view linked physical returns to eBay case data.
operationsPrograms.approach: Built the Daily Returns Board with SLA enforcement used across the organisation. Companion Returns Label Scanner in progress: reads printed return labels, calls eBay Returns API, feeds structured actionable tasks with automatic SLA deadlines.
highlights: Org-wide Daily Returns Board with SLA enforcement — replaces ad-hoc tracking
Returns Label Scanner eliminates manual transcription from label to case
eBay Returns API integration pulls buyer reason, tracking, and case status automatically
Structured tasks with deadlines — no return missed or delayed through handoff gaps

screenshots.caption: Daily Returns Board — SLA view
screenshots.capture: Daily Returns Board showing open returns, SLA status, and team assignments. Redact buyer PII.

screenshots.caption: Returns Label Scanner — API intake
screenshots.capture: Label scanner app after reading a return label — show eBay return data pulled via API and task created with SLA deadline.

operationsPrograms.title: Custom trade-in platform
operationsPrograms.problem: Trade-in was a core inventory flywheel but no off-the-shelf product matched RebX grading, ops, or GDPR requirements.
operationsPrograms.approach: Conceived, built, and delivered entirely in-house with no external development — bespoke to RebX operations with encrypted customer data.
highlights: End-to-end trade-in valuation on rebx.co.uk — feeds refurbished inventory pipeline
GDPR-compliant with customer data encrypted to industry best practice
No agency or external developer — sole design and delivery

highlights: Sole owner concept-to-delivery — dual Refurbished + Bargains shops in one cohesive storefront
Custom Liquid theme, collection architecture, StoreFeeder integration, payments
Custom trade-in platform built in-house on the same stack
